text.title: Design Ops Foundations Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Design Ops Foundations application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Three rituals: weekly critique (2h), biweekly system review (1h), monthly capacity check (1h). Rituals are mandatory; not 'when we have time'.
rule.rationale: Industry DesignOps consensus (NN/g, DesignOps Assembly): three rituals = the floor of a functional design team.

rule.statement: Design system + tokens + components live in ONE source (Figma library, Tokens Studio, or coded design system). Drift across sources is the most common ops failure.
rule.rationale: Multi-source design system silently fragments; one source + sync is the only sustainable model.

rule.statement: Capacity model in design-points (not hours): epic = 5-13 points, feature = 2-8, polish = 1-3. Estimate at sprint start; recalibrate quarterly.
rule.rationale: Hours-based estimation collapses under interruption; point-based estimation survives.

rule.statement: Every design ship has an engineering review BEFORE handoff (≥30 min). Catches infeasible designs before they consume engineering time.
rule.rationale: Designs that look good but are infeasible cost 5-10× to discover post-handoff; pre-handoff review is the cheapest fix.

rule.statement: Quarterly review of design-tool inventory: drop tools used by <50% of team, consolidate overlapping tools, document the rationale.
rule.rationale: Tool sprawl is the silent ops killer; quarterly rationalization keeps stack lean + onboarding fast.
